title: Makhani Gravy

ingredients.title: Main Body
items: Cashew nuts – 80 g, soaked in warm water for 20 minutes

ingredients.title: Finishing & Balancing
items: Kasuri methi – 3 g, toasted and crushed

instruction.title: 1. Heat & Temper
steps: Heat oil and 25 g butter in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add green cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf, and black cardamom.
Sauté for 30–40 seconds until fragrant.

instruction.title: 2. Build the Base
steps: Add chopped ginger and garlic.
Sauté gently without browning.
Add tomatoes, soaked cashews, salt, and Kashmiri red chili powder.
Mix thoroughly.

instruction.title: 3. Simmer Gently
steps: Cover and cook on medium-low heat for 30–40 minutes.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Cook until tomatoes are completely soft and the oil begins to separate.

instruction.title: 4. Blend to Silk
steps: Remove whole spices if desired.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly.
Blend into a very smooth puree.
For a restaurant-quality finish, strain through a fine sieve or chinois.

instruction.title: 5. Refine & Finish
steps: Return the puree to the pot.
Add honey or sugar.
Add the remaining 25 g butter and fresh cream.
Add toasted crushed kasuri methi.
Simmer gently for 5–7 minutes.
Adjust seasoning and sweetness as needed.

instruction.title: 6. Final Gloss
steps: Finish each serving with a swirl of fresh cream.
Add a small knob of butter before serving.

chefTips: Tomato quality is crucial; use ripe, naturally sweet tomatoes for the best flavor.
Avoid boiling the gravy after adding cream to prevent splitting.
Straining the blended gravy creates an exceptionally smooth restaurant-style texture.
Cashews provide body and richness without needing excessive cream.
Store refrigerated for up to 4 days in an airtight container.
Freeze in portions for up to 2 months.
Use as a base for Butter Chicken, Paneer Makhani, Butter Paneer Masala, Dal Makhani, or vegetable makhani curries.
For extra richness, finish with a touch of butter and cream just before service.
